author: bharath.vedartham
content: why would builders need to create a circuit breaker here? if their payload is not successfully revealed, wouldn't the chain have to require a circuit breaker?

author: bharath.vedartham
content: ofc based on some heursitic like, if the payload is not revealed for N consecutive slots or N slots in an epoch

author: 0xunclebill
content:
because a faulty builder would be losing money if they don’t circuit break themselves before the in protocol one is triggered  

It would be better if the builders circuit breaker themselves. When we build our trustless builder client I think we’ll add a configurable circuit breaker

author: bharath.vedartham
content:
true but if a payload doesn't make it on chain, its likely the builders fault right? 
ahh I can see how this plays out:
Builder infra has a reveal bug and the builder wins multiple blocks in a row. But none of these reveals happen so you just stop serving/sending bids until the  bug is fixed

author: bharath.vedartham
content: in this case, the chain should also have a circuit breaker right? to protect against liveness if multiple payloads are not being revealed

author: 0xunclebill
content: yeah we should still have the chain circuit breaker but ideally builders have their own that trigger before our chain circuit breaker

author: 0xunclebill
content: we’d rather have builders catching their own issues before the protocol needs to intervene
